titles.title: Captain America: The First Avenger
titles.focus: Steve Rogers' values BEFORE the powers — the man earns the shield. Watch the Tesseract (first Infinity Stone) and the birth of HYDRA.
titles.point: Chronological ground zero. Plants the Tesseract/Space Stone, HYDRA, and the moral compass the whole saga is measured against.
titles.overview: During World War II, Steve Rogers is a sickly man from Brooklyn who's transformed into super-soldier Captain America to aid in the war effort. Rogers must stop the Red Skull – Adolf Hitler's ruthless head of weaponry, and the leader of an organization that intends to use a mysterious device of untold powers for world domination.

titles.title: Captain Marvel
titles.focus: How Fury loses his eye and gets the idea for the Avengers. The Tesseract resurfaces and the Skrull/Kree war is set up.
titles.point: Origin of the 'Avengers Initiative' name and the most powerful hero, parked in the '90s so her Endgame return lands.
titles.overview: The story follows Carol Danvers as she becomes one of the universe’s most powerful heroes when Earth is caught in the middle of a galactic war between two alien races. Set in the 1990s, Captain Marvel is an all-new adventure from a previously unseen period in the history of the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

titles.title: Iron Man
titles.focus: Tony Stark's arc from arms dealer to hero, and the tone of the whole universe. Stay for the Nick Fury post-credit scene.
titles.point: The film that started everything. Establishes the MCU's grounded-tech tone and the 'Avengers Initiative' tease.
titles.overview: After being held captive in an Afghan cave, billionaire engineer Tony Stark creates a unique weaponized suit of armor to fight evil.

titles.title: The Incredible Hulk
titles.focus: Bruce Banner on the run and the super-soldier programme link. The most skippable entry — note Tony's cameo at the end.
titles.point: Connects HYDRA's super-soldier obsession to the present and slots Hulk into the wider world. Often skipped without much loss.
titles.overview: Scientist Bruce Banner scours the planet for an antidote to the unbridled force of rage within him: the Hulk. But when the military masterminds who dream of exploiting his powers force him back to civilization, he finds himself coming face to face with a new, deadly foe.

titles.title: Iron Man 2
titles.focus: Black Widow's introduction, the birth of S.H.I.E.L.D. on screen, and Tony confronting his own mortality.
titles.point: World-building bridge: introduces Natasha, expands S.H.I.E.L.D., and teases Thor's hammer in the post-credit.
titles.overview: With the world now aware of his dual life as the armored superhero Iron Man, billionaire inventor Tony Stark faces pressure from the government, the press and the public to share his technology with the military. Unwilling to let go of his invention, Stark, with Pepper Potts and James 'Rhodey' Rhodes at his side, must forge new alliances – and confront powerful enemies.

titles.title: Thor
titles.focus: The cosmic/mythic side of the MCU and Loki's debut as the saga's best villain. The Tesseract returns in the post-credit.
titles.point: Opens the universe beyond Earth and launches Loki — the antagonist who drives the first Avengers film.
titles.overview: Against his father Odin's will, The Mighty Thor - a powerful but arrogant warrior god - recklessly reignites an ancient war. Thor is cast down to Earth and forced to live among humans as punishment. Once here, Thor learns what it takes to be a true hero when the most dangerous villain of his world sends the darkest forces of Asgard to invade Earth.

titles.title: The Avengers
titles.focus: The first team-up and how each hero's ego is sanded down into a unit. The Mind Stone (in Loki's sceptre) and Thanos reveal.
titles.point: Pays off Phase 1. The Battle of New York is referenced for a DECADE and the Thanos post-credit starts the endgame.
titles.overview: When an unexpected enemy emerges and threatens global safety and security, Nick Fury, director of the international peacekeeping agency known as S.H.I.E.L.D., finds himself in need of a team to pull the world back from the brink of disaster. Spanning the globe, a daring recruitment effort begins!

titles.title: Iron Man 3
titles.focus: Tony's PTSD after New York — the first real 'consequences' film. The Mandarin twist divides fans for a reason.
titles.point: Shows the human cost of being a hero and sets up the anxiety that drives Tony toward Ultron.
titles.overview: When Tony Stark's world is torn apart by a formidable terrorist called the Mandarin, he starts an odyssey of rebuilding and retribution.

titles.title: Thor: The Dark World
titles.focus: Mostly the Aether — note it's the Reality Stone in liquid form. Loki's growth matters more than the plot.
titles.point: Quietly delivers a second Infinity Stone (Reality) into play. Skippable for plot, but the Stone bookkeeping counts.
titles.overview: Thor fights to restore order across the cosmos… but an ancient race led by the vengeful Malekith returns to plunge the universe back into darkness. Faced with an enemy that even Odin and Asgard cannot withstand, Thor must embark on his most perilous and personal journey yet, one that will reunite him with Jane Foster and force him to sacrifice everything to save us all.

titles.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
titles.focus: HYDRA's secret takeover of S.H.I.E.L.D. and Bucky's return. A '70s political thriller in a cape.
titles.point: A franchise turning point: dismantles S.H.I.E.L.D., reshaping every story that follows. Widely the best-reviewed entry.
titles.overview: After the cataclysmic events in New York with The Avengers, Steve Rogers, aka Captain America is living quietly in Washington, D.C. and trying to adjust to the modern world. But when a S.H.I.E.L.D. colleague comes under attack, Steve becomes embroiled in a web of intrigue that threatens to put the world at risk. Joining forces with the Black Widow, Captain America struggles to expose the ever-widening conspiracy while fighting off professional assassins sent to silence him at every turn. When the full scope of the villainous plot is revealed, Captain America and the Black Widow enlist the help of a new ally, the Falcon. However, they soon find themselves up against an unexpected and formidable enemy—the Winter Soldier.

titles.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
titles.focus: The cosmic outlaws and the Power Stone — note Thanos and the Collector tie this directly to the bigger plan.
titles.point: Expands the galaxy far from Earth and locates a third Infinity Stone, setting the cosmic stage for Thanos.
titles.overview: Light years from Earth, 26 years after being abducted, Peter Quill finds himself the prime target of a manhunt after discovering an orb wanted by Ronan the Accuser.

titles.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
titles.focus: Quill's father Ego and the team becoming a family. Mostly character work — light on saga plot.
titles.point: Deepens the Guardians as people so their Infinity War / Endgame stakes hit harder. Introduces Mantis and Adam Warlock tease.
titles.overview: The Guardians must fight to keep their newfound family together as they unravel the mysteries of Peter Quill's true parentage.

titles.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron
titles.focus: Vision's creation from the Mind Stone, Wanda & Pietro's debut, and the cracks forming between Tony and Steve.
titles.point: Manufactures Vision (Mind Stone made flesh) and seeds the ideological split that explodes in Civil War.
titles.overview: When Tony Stark tries to jumpstart a dormant peacekeeping program, things go awry and Earth’s Mightiest Heroes are put to the ultimate test as the fate of the planet hangs in the balance. As the villainous Ultron emerges, it is up to The Avengers to stop him from enacting his terrible plans, and soon uneasy alliances and unexpected action pave the way for an epic and unique global adventure.

titles.title: Ant-Man
titles.focus: The Quantum Realm — keep it in mind, it literally saves the universe later. A small-stakes heist palate cleanser.
titles.point: Introduces the Quantum Realm, the mechanism behind Endgame's time heist, and a relatable street-level hero.
titles.overview: Armed with the astonishing ability to shrink in scale but increase in strength, master thief Scott Lang must embrace his inner-hero and help his mentor, Doctor Hank Pym, protect the secret behind his spectacular Ant-Man suit from a new generation of towering threats. Against seemingly insurmountable obstacles, Pym and Lang must plan and pull off a heist that will save the world.

titles.title: Captain America: Civil War
titles.focus: The Avengers split over accountability. Introductions of Black Panther and Spider-Man inside an emotional war.
titles.point: Fractures the team right before Thanos arrives — the heroes enter Infinity War divided and weaker. Pivotal.
titles.overview: Following the events of Age of Ultron, the collective governments of the world pass an act designed to regulate all superhuman activity. This polarizes opinion amongst the Avengers, causing two factions to side with Iron Man or Captain America, which causes an epic battle between former allies.

titles.title: Black Panther
titles.focus: Wakanda, vibranium, and Killmonger — the most thematically rich villain. Note Wakanda becomes the Endgame battlefield.
titles.point: Builds the nation and tech that host the final stand, and gives the saga its cultural high-water mark.
titles.overview: King T'Challa returns home to the reclusive, technologically advanced African nation of Wakanda to serve as his country's new leader. However, T'Challa soon finds that he is challenged for the throne by factions within his own country as well as without. Using powers reserved to Wakandan kings, T'Challa assumes the Black Panther mantle to join with ex-girlfriend Nakia, the queen-mother, his princess-kid sister, members of the Dora Milaje (the Wakandan 'special forces') and an American secret agent, to prevent Wakanda from being dragged into a world war.

titles.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
titles.focus: Peter mentored by Tony — the father/son bond that powers his later grief. Street-level stakes after a cosmic war.
titles.point: Establishes the Stark–Parker relationship that becomes the emotional core of the Phase 3 finale and beyond.
titles.overview: Following the events of Captain America: Civil War, Peter Parker, with the help of his mentor Tony Stark, tries to balance his life as an ordinary high school student in Queens, New York City, with fighting crime as his superhero alter ego Spider-Man as a new threat, the Vulture, emerges.

titles.title: Doctor Strange
titles.focus: Magic and the Time Stone (the Eye of Agamotto). The multiverse and 'looking forward in time' are seeded here.
titles.point: Adds sorcery and the final Infinity Stone (Time) to the board — Strange's foresight decides Endgame.
titles.overview: After his career is destroyed, a brilliant but arrogant surgeon gets a new lease on life when a sorcerer takes him under her wing and trains him to defend the world against evil.

titles.title: Black Widow
titles.focus: Natasha's family and the Red Room — a prequel filling the gap after Civil War. Introduces Yelena, her successor.
titles.point: A send-off and hand-off: closes Natasha's backstory and launches Yelena Belova into the next generation.
titles.overview: Natasha Romanoff, also known as Black Widow, confronts the darker parts of her ledger when a dangerous conspiracy with ties to her past arises. Pursued by a force that will stop at nothing to bring her down, Natasha must deal with her history as a spy and the broken relationships left in her wake long before she became an Avenger.

titles.title: Thor: Ragnarok
titles.focus: Thor reinvented as comedy, the loss of Asgard, and Hela. The ending ship is ambushed at the start of Infinity War.
titles.point: Strips Thor down (no hammer, no home) and leaves Asgardians in space — the exact target of Infinity War's opening.
titles.overview: Thor is imprisoned on the other side of the universe and finds himself in a race against time to get back to Asgard to stop Ragnarok, the destruction of his home-world and the end of Asgardian civilization, at the hands of a powerful new threat, the ruthless Hela.

titles.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
titles.focus: Runs PARALLEL to Infinity War — that's why they're absent from it. The post-credit Snap is the gut-punch.
titles.point: Keeps Scott in the Quantum Realm at the moment of the Snap, the setup for his Endgame return with the time-heist idea.
titles.overview: Just when his time under house arrest is about to end, Scott Lang once again puts his freedom at risk to help Hope van Dyne and Dr. Hank Pym dive into the quantum realm and try to accomplish, against time and any chance of success, a very dangerous rescue mission.

titles.title: Avengers: Infinity War
titles.focus: Thanos as the protagonist of his own film. Every Stone converges — and the heroes LOSE. Watch who dusts.
titles.point: The saga's villain wins. The Snap is the single most consequential event in the MCU; everything after reacts to it.
titles.overview: As the Avengers and their allies have continued to protect the world from threats too large for any one hero to handle, a new danger has emerged from the cosmic shadows: Thanos. A despot of intergalactic infamy, his goal is to collect all six Infinity Stones, artifacts of unimaginable power, and use them to inflict his twisted will on all of reality. Everything the Avengers have fought for has led up to this moment - the fate of Earth and existence itself has never been more uncertain.

titles.title: Avengers: Endgame
titles.focus: Grief, the time heist, and the payoff of 11 years. Cap's arc, Tony's sacrifice, the 5-year 'Blip' jump.
titles.point: The Infinity Saga's finale. Resets the roster, ages the timeline 5 years (the Blip), and clears the deck for Phase 4.
titles.overview: After the devastating events of Avengers: Infinity War, the universe is in ruins due to the efforts of the Mad Titan, Thanos. With the help of remaining allies, the Avengers must assemble once more in order to undo Thanos' actions and restore order to the universe once and for all, no matter what consequences may be in store.

titles.title: WandaVision
titles.focus: Grief weaponised. Wanda's powers and the 'Scarlet Witch' title emerge — essential before Multiverse of Madness.
titles.point: Opens the Multiverse Saga's emotional and magical threads, and sets Wanda on the path to villainy in Doctor Strange 2.
titles.overview: Wanda Maximoff and Vision—two super-powered beings living idealized suburban lives—begin to suspect that everything is not as it seems.

titles.title: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier
titles.focus: Who deserves the shield after Steve? The Blip's political fallout and Sam earning the mantle of Captain America.
titles.point: Hands the shield to Sam and seeds the new Cap and the Thunderbolts-adjacent politics of Phase 5.
titles.overview: Following the events of “Avengers: Endgame”, the Falcon, Sam Wilson and the Winter Soldier, Bucky Barnes team up in a global adventure that tests their abilities, and their patience.

titles.title: Loki
titles.focus: The TVA, the Sacred Timeline, and Kang ('He Who Remains'). The single most important watch for the Multiverse Saga.
titles.point: Literally breaks the timeline open — the multiverse and Kang both begin here. The backbone of the whole saga.
titles.overview: After stealing the Tesseract during the events of “Avengers: Endgame,” an alternate version of Loki is brought to the mysterious Time Variance Authority, a bureaucratic organization that exists outside of time and space and monitors the timeline. They give Loki a choice: face being erased from existence due to being a “time variant” or help fix the timeline and stop a greater threat.

titles.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
titles.focus: A fresh, martial-arts-driven hero and the mystery of the Ten Rings — a signal/beacon teased for the future.
titles.point: Introduces a new flagship hero and the Ten Rings, a slow-burn mystery being seeded across the new saga.
titles.overview: Shang-Chi must confront the past he thought he left behind when he is drawn into the web of the mysterious Ten Rings organization.

titles.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
titles.focus: Life after Tony — Peter inheriting the burden. Mysterio frames Spider-Man and reveals his identity in the post-credit.
titles.point: Closes the Infinity Saga and sets the identity-crisis cliffhanger that No Way Home resolves.
titles.overview: Peter Parker and his friends go on a summer trip to Europe. However, they will hardly be able to rest - Peter will have to agree to help Nick Fury uncover the mystery of creatures that cause natural disasters and destruction throughout the continent.

titles.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
titles.focus: The multiverse made personal — villains and Spideys cross over. Strange's spell is the hinge of the whole event.
titles.point: First big multiverse crossover for general audiences; proves the saga's central gimmick and resets Peter's world.
titles.overview: Peter Parker is unmasked and no longer able to separate his normal life from the high-stakes of being a super-hero. When he asks for help from Doctor Strange the stakes become even more dangerous, forcing him to discover what it truly means to be Spider-Man.

titles.title: Hawkeye
titles.focus: Clint passing the torch to Kate Bishop, and Yelena's grief arc continuing. Kingpin returns to the MCU.
titles.point: Builds the next-gen roster (Kate, Yelena) and reintroduces the street-level crime world (Kingpin, Echo).
titles.overview: Former Avenger Clint Barton has a seemingly simple mission: get back to his family for Christmas. Possible? Maybe with the help of Kate Bishop, a 22-year-old archer with dreams of becoming a superhero. The two are forced to work together when a presence from Barton’s past threatens to derail far more than the festive spirit.

titles.title: Ms. Marvel
titles.focus: A fan-first teen hero, and a quiet but huge reveal about her powers' mutant/cosmic nature. Leads straight into The Marvels.
titles.point: Introduces Kamala Khan and drops the franchise's first on-screen 'mutation' hint — a bridge toward the X-Men era.
titles.overview: A great student, avid gamer, and voracious fan-fic scribe, Kamala Khan has a special affinity for superheroes, particularly Captain Marvel. However, she struggles to fit in at home and at school — that is, until she gets superpowers like the heroes she’s always looked up to. Life is easier with superpowers, right?

titles.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
titles.focus: Wanda's WandaVision grief turned villainous, America Chavez, and incursions. WandaVision is required viewing first.
titles.point: Pays off Wanda's arc, formalises multiverse-travel rules and 'incursions', the saga's looming catastrophe.
titles.overview: Doctor Strange, with the help of mystical allies both old and new, traverses the mind-bending and dangerous alternate realities of the Multiverse to confront a mysterious new adversary.

titles.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
titles.focus: Grief for T'Challa woven into the real loss of Chadwick Boseman. Namor and the underwater nation Talokan debut.
titles.point: Recasts the mantle, introduces Namor/Talokan as a major new power, and closes Phase 4 on an elegiac note.
titles.overview: Queen Ramonda, Shuri, M’Baku, Okoye and the Dora Milaje fight to protect their nation from intervening world powers in the wake of King T’Challa’s death.  As the Wakandans strive to embrace their next chapter, the heroes must band together with the help of War Dog Nakia and Everett Ross and forge a new path for the kingdom of Wakanda.

titles.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
titles.focus: The Fox X-Men universe colliding with the MCU via the TVA and the Void. Loki's multiverse rules pay off comedically.
titles.point: Officially folds the X-Men/Fox legacy into the MCU multiverse — the clearest bridge to mutants joining the saga.
titles.overview: A listless Wade Wilson toils away in civilian life with his days as the morally flexible mercenary, Deadpool, behind him. But when his homeworld faces an existential threat, Wade must reluctantly suit-up again with an even more reluctant Wolverine.

titles.title: Thunderbolts*
titles.focus: Antiheroes (Yelena, Bucky, US Agent, Ghost) forced into a team — the 'dark Avengers' beginning to form.
titles.point: Assembles the morally-grey roster that fills the Avengers vacuum heading into the saga's culmination.
titles.overview: After finding themselves ensnared in a death trap, seven disillusioned castoffs must embark on a dangerous mission that will force them to confront the darkest corners of their pasts.

titles.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
titles.focus: Marvel's First Family arrives in the MCU on a retro-futuristic alternate Earth. Watch how they're set up ahead of the big crossovers.
titles.point: Introduces the Fantastic Four (and Galactus) just before Doomsday — fresh players entering the Secret Wars endgame.
titles.overview: Against the vibrant backdrop of a 1960s-inspired, retro-futuristic world, Marvel's First Family is forced to balance their roles as heroes with the strength of their family bond, while defending Earth from a ravenous space god called Galactus and his enigmatic Herald, Silver Surfer.

titles.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
titles.focus: Peter Parker's life after the memory-wipe of No Way Home — a street-level reset before the multiverse peaks.
titles.point: Re-centres Spider-Man for the new era and is expected to feed straight into the Avengers two-parter.
titles.overview: Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn't remember him—and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him—sparks a change in Peter Parker he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves - a powerful villain no one can even see.

titles.title: Avengers: Doomsday
titles.focus: Doctor Doom takes centre stage as the saga's big bad, pulling heroes — and the X-Men — from across the multiverse together.
titles.point: The Multiverse Saga's Infinity-War-scale convergence; the incursion threads finally collide.
titles.overview: Beloved heroes from three distinct universes are set on a deadly collision course and face an existential threat unlike anything they've ever encountered.

titles.title: Avengers: Secret Wars
titles.focus: The culmination — realities collapse into a patchwork 'Battleworld'. Expect the biggest crossover yet.
titles.point: The Endgame of the Multiverse Saga; likely resets the MCU and fuses the X-Men and MCU lines for good.
titles.overview: An upcoming film in Phase Six of the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) and the finale of The Multiverse Saga. Plot TBA.

titles.title: X-Men
titles.focus: The film that launched the modern superhero era. Focus on the mutant-as-outsider allegory and the Xavier/Magneto rivalry.
titles.point: Origin of the whole X-Men franchise now folding into the MCU — establishes the core mutant mythology.
titles.overview: Two mutants, Rogue and Wolverine, come to a private academy for their kind whose resident superhero team, the X-Men, must oppose a terrorist organization with similar powers.

titles.title: X2: X-Men United
titles.focus: Heroes and villains forced to team up against a human threat. Widely the best of the original trilogy.
titles.point: Deepens the mutant-rights theme and seeds the Dark Phoenix arc.
titles.overview: Professor Charles Xavier and his team of genetically gifted superheroes face a rising tide of anti-mutant sentiment led by Col. William Stryker. Storm, Wolverine and Jean Grey must join Magneto and Mystique to unhinge Stryker's scheme to exterminate all mutants.

titles.title: X-Men: The Last Stand
titles.focus: The 'mutant cure' and an unleashed Dark Phoenix. Divisive, but pivotal for the trilogy's stakes.
titles.point: Closes the original trilogy and motivates the later timeline reset.
titles.overview: When a cure is found to treat mutations, lines are drawn amongst the X-Men led by Professor Charles Xavier, and Magneto's Brotherhood of Mutants.

titles.title: X-Men Origins: Wolverine
titles.focus: Logan and Victor's backstory and the birth of Weapon X. Note the infamous first take on Deadpool.
titles.point: Fills in Wolverine's adamantium origin — context for Logan and Deadpool & Wolverine.
titles.overview: After seeking to live a normal life after 200 years, Logan sets out to avenge a death by undergoing the mutant Weapon X program and becoming Wolverine.

titles.title: X-Men: First Class
titles.focus: A '60s reboot — young Charles and Erik's friendship and split. The franchise's strongest character work.
titles.point: Restarts the timeline with a new cast and launches the Days of Future Past saga.
titles.overview: Before Charles Xavier and Erik Lensherr took the names Professor X and Magneto, they were two young men discovering their powers for the first time. Before they were arch-enemies, they were closest of friends, working together with other mutants (some familiar, some new), to stop the greatest threat the world has ever known.

titles.title: The Wolverine
titles.focus: Logan in Japan, stripped of his healing factor — a more personal, grounded story.
titles.point: Bridges the original trilogy to Days of Future Past (watch the post-credits).
titles.overview: Wolverine faces his ultimate nemesis - and tests of his physical, emotional, and mortal limits - in a life-changing voyage to modern-day Japan.

titles.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past
titles.focus: Time travel uniting both casts. The hinge that rewrites the franchise's broken continuity.
titles.point: Resets the timeline (erasing The Last Stand) — the X-films' own 'Endgame' moment.
titles.overview: The ultimate X-Men ensemble fights a war for the survival of the species across two time periods as they join forces with their younger selves in an epic battle that must change the past – to save our future.

titles.title: Deadpool
titles.focus: The R-rated, fourth-wall-breaking antihero. A tonal outlier that became a franchise.
titles.point: Proved R-rated comic films work; sets up Deadpool & Wolverine's MCU crossover.
titles.overview: The origin story of former Special Forces operative turned mercenary Wade Wilson, who, after being subjected to a rogue experiment that leaves him with accelerated healing powers, adopts the alter ego Deadpool. Armed with his new abilities and a dark, twisted sense of humor, Deadpool hunts down the man who nearly destroyed his life.

titles.title: X-Men: Apocalypse
titles.focus: An ancient mutant god-villain, with the young cast in the '80s and a standout Quicksilver sequence.
titles.point: Continues the rebooted timeline toward Dark Phoenix.
titles.overview: After the re-emergence of the world's first mutant, world-destroyer Apocalypse, the X-Men must unite to defeat his extinction level plan.

titles.title: Logan
titles.focus: An elegiac, near-future send-off for Wolverine. The franchise's most acclaimed film.
titles.point: A definitive Wolverine farewell and proof of the X-films' dramatic range.
titles.overview: In the near future, a weary Logan cares for an ailing Professor X in a hideout on the Mexican border. But Logan's attempts to hide from the world and his legacy are upended when a young mutant arrives, pursued by dark forces.

titles.title: Deadpool 2
titles.focus: Cable, Domino, and the X-Force, with more meta humour and heart.
titles.point: Expands Deadpool's world and the time-travel gags that pay off in Deadpool & Wolverine.
titles.overview: Wisecracking mercenary Deadpool battles the evil and powerful Cable and other bad guys to save a boy's life.

titles.title: Dark Phoenix
titles.focus: A second crack at the Phoenix saga and the final film of the Fox X-Men era.
titles.point: Ends the Fox X-Men continuity right before Disney's acquisition folded mutants into the MCU.
titles.overview: The X-Men face their most formidable and powerful foe when one of their own, Jean Grey, starts to spiral out of control. During a rescue mission in outer space, Jean is nearly killed when she's hit by a mysterious cosmic force. Once she returns home, this force not only makes her infinitely more powerful, but far more unstable. The X-Men must now band together to save her soul and battle aliens that want to use Grey's new abilities to rule the galaxy.

titles.title: The New Mutants
titles.focus: A horror-tinged spin-off with young mutants trapped in a facility. The very last Fox X-Men release.
titles.point: The franchise's quiet closer before the X-Men's MCU reboot.
titles.overview: Five young mutants, just discovering their abilities while held in a secret facility against their will, fight to escape their past sins and save themselves.

titles.title: Spider-Man
titles.focus: The blueprint for the modern superhero origin — Peter, the Green Goblin, and 'with great power…'.
titles.point: Launched Spider-Man on film and kick-started the 2000s comic-movie boom.
titles.overview: After being bitten by a genetically altered spider at Oscorp, nerdy but endearing high school student Peter Parker is endowed with amazing powers to become the superhero known as Spider-Man.

titles.title: Spider-Man 2
titles.focus: Doc Ock and the cost of the double life. Long held up as one of the best superhero films.
titles.point: The gold-standard sequel; defined the 'hero gives up the mask' arc.
titles.overview: Peter Parker is going through a major identity crisis. Burned out from being Spider-Man, he decides to shelve his superhero alter ego, which leaves the city suffering in the wake of carnage left by the evil Doc Ock. In the meantime, Parker still can't act on his feelings for Mary Jane Watson, a girl he's loved since childhood. A certain anger begins to brew in his best friend Harry Osborn as well...

titles.title: Spider-Man 3
titles.focus: Venom, Sandman and the symbiote — overstuffed but ambitious. The 'emo Peter' meme lives here.
titles.point: Closes the Raimi trilogy; its Venom misfire shaped Sony's later plans.
titles.overview: The seemingly invincible Spider-Man goes up against an all-new crop of villains—including the shape-shifting Sandman. While Spider-Man’s superpowers are altered by an alien organism, his alter ego, Peter Parker, deals with nemesis Eddie Brock and also gets caught up in a love triangle.

titles.title: The Amazing Spider-Man
titles.focus: A grittier reboot with the Lizard and Gwen Stacy. Andrew Garfield's wittier Spidey.
titles.point: Sony's reboot to keep the rights; Garfield's arc returns years later in No Way Home.
titles.overview: Peter Parker is an outcast high schooler abandoned by his parents as a boy, leaving him to be raised by his Uncle Ben and Aunt May. Like most teenagers, Peter is trying to figure out who he is and how he got to be the person he is today. As Peter discovers a mysterious briefcase that belonged to his father, he begins a quest to understand his parents' disappearance – leading him directly to Oscorp and the lab of Dr. Curt Connors, his father's former partner. As Spider-Man is set on a collision course with Connors' alter ego, The Lizard, Peter will make life-altering choices to use his powers and shape his destiny to become a hero.

titles.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
titles.focus: Electro, the Goblin and a gut-punch Gwen Stacy ending. Overloaded with universe-building.
titles.point: Its franchise collapse pushed Sony toward the MCU deal and the SSU.
titles.overview: For Peter Parker, life is busy. Between taking out the bad guys as Spider-Man and spending time with the person he loves, Gwen Stacy, high school graduation cannot come quickly enough. Peter has not forgotten about the promise he made to Gwen’s father to protect her by staying away, but that is a promise he cannot keep. Things will change for Peter when a new villain, Electro, emerges, an old friend, Harry Osborn, returns, and Peter uncovers new clues about his past.

titles.title: Venom
titles.focus: Eddie Brock and the symbiote as a buddy-comedy antihero. Tom Hardy's dual performance.
titles.point: Launches Sony's Spider-Man Universe — a roster of Spidey villains, minus Spider-Man.
titles.overview: Investigative journalist Eddie Brock attempts a comeback following a scandal, but accidentally becomes the host of Venom, a violent, super powerful alien symbiote. Soon, he must rely on his newfound powers to protect the world from a shadowy organization looking for a symbiote of their own.

titles.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
titles.focus: Carnage, and the Venom/Eddie odd-couple dynamic. Stay for the MCU-tying mid-credits.
titles.point: Bridges the SSU to the MCU multiverse (the Venom cameo in No Way Home).
titles.overview: After finding a host body in investigative reporter Eddie Brock, the alien symbiote must face a new enemy, Carnage, the alter ego of serial killer Cletus Kasady.

titles.title: Morbius
titles.focus: The living-vampire antihero — infamous for its memes and confusing post-credits.
titles.point: Expands the SSU's villain roster; widely panned.
titles.overview: Dangerously ill with a rare blood disorder, and determined to save others suffering his same fate, Dr. Michael Morbius attempts a desperate gamble. What at first appears to be a radical success soon reveals itself to be a remedy potentially worse than the disease.

titles.title: Madame Web
titles.focus: A clairvoyant origin set in 2003, introducing future Spider-Women. Another SSU experiment.
titles.point: Tries to widen the SSU beyond Venom; a critical and box-office stumble.
titles.overview: Forced to confront revelations about her past, paramedic Cassandra Webb forges a relationship with three young women destined for powerful futures...if they can all survive a deadly present.

titles.title: Venom: The Last Dance
titles.focus: The finale of the Venom trilogy — Eddie and Venom's last road trip.
titles.point: Closes out the SSU's flagship Venom arc.
titles.overview: Eddie and Venom are on the run. Hunted by both of their worlds and with the net closing in, the duo are forced into a devastating decision that will bring the curtains down on Venom and Eddie's last dance.

titles.title: Kraven the Hunter
titles.focus: An R-rated origin for the big-game hunter villain.
titles.point: One of the last SSU entries before Sony reassessed the universe.
titles.overview: Kraven Kravinoff's complex relationship with his ruthless gangster father, Nikolai, starts him down a path of vengeance with brutal consequences, motivating him to become not only the greatest hunter in the world, but also one of its most feared.

titles.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
titles.focus: Miles Morales and a groundbreaking animation style — the film that made the multiverse cool.
titles.point: Oscar-winning; popularised the multiverse concept the MCU later leaned into.
titles.overview: Struggling to find his place in the world while juggling school and family, Brooklyn teenager Miles Morales is unexpectedly bitten by a radioactive spider and develops unfathomable powers just like the one and only Spider-Man. While wrestling with the implications of his new abilities, Miles discovers a super collider created by the madman Wilson "Kingpin" Fisk, causing others from across the Spider-Verse to be inadvertently transported to his dimension.

titles.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
titles.focus: Miles vs. the Spider-Society and the Spot. A visual leap and a brutal cliffhanger.
titles.point: Expands the animated multiverse; the definitive Spider-Verse statement.
titles.overview: After reuniting with Gwen Stacy, Brooklyn’s full-time, friendly neighborhood Spider-Man is catapulted across the Multiverse, where he encounters the Spider Society, a team of Spider-People charged with protecting the Multiverse's very existence. But when the heroes clash on how to handle a new threat, Miles finds himself pitted against the other Spiders and must set out on his own to save those he loves most.
